pages.raw_text:
The ticket brokers got a hard jolt
at the meeting of the city council
last evening when Councilman Lind
say (the tool of the railroads) intro
duced an ordinance requiring all
brokers to pay a license Of 500 a
year and put up bond of $4,000. The
ordinance was presented through the
efforts of the chamber of commerce,
who wish to put stop to the prac-
tice.


The ordinance requires that the
term ticket broker shall apply to all
who deal in tickets unless they are
connected with a steamship or rail-
way line. The brokers will be re-
quired to make daily reports to the
chief Of police, in which will be in
cluded every sale and purchase, to-
gether with a description of the
ticket.


Upon failure to do so the broker
is liable to fine of from $50 to $500
and Imprisonment not to exceed &c
days.


Pound Limits.


The city pound limits were greatly
extended and cover practically the en-
tire city. The ordinance was passed
last night The ordinance prohibits
the driving of herds larger than II
through the city without a permit
from the mayor and prohibits any
stock from running loose in thu
pound limits under any circum
stances Stock must be kept tiet
up in any portion of the city whether


the hours Of s p. m. and o'clocl
a. m.


Councilman Gray introduced an or
dinance which provides that hereafte
all sidewalks in the city shall b.
built of either cement or vitrifiet


brick. The aim of the ordinance il
to do away with the wooden side
walks.


The council will meet the commit
tee of the Taxpayers league on Fri
day and will then go over the watel
| situation with them. Engineer Gil
| submitted plans for a reservoir OIL
| Cooks hill. He also submitted plan.
| for a 1000, 900 gallon reservoir to b.
| built in Lidgerwood.
The remainder of the business o
| the council was routine work and thu
passing of street improvement ordi
| nances. A few saloon licenses wer
granted.
